name: Radicle
description: Radicle — Peer-to-peer code collaboration protocol built on Git for sovereign, decentralized OSS development workflows.

seo.title: Radicle — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: Radicle: Radicle — Peer-to-peer code collaboration protocol built on Git for sovereign, decentralized OSS development workflows.

when_to_use: P2P Git collaboration without GitHub dependency
Sovereign code hosting for privacy-focused OSS projects
Decentralized issue and patch management on Git
Self-sovereign code history without cloud lock-in

pros: Truly decentralized, no central server to trust
Git-native, so your workflow barely changes
Token incentives for node operators exist
cons: Discovery and social features are still basic
Much smaller network effect than GitHub
review.verdict: The most credible decentralized code host; promising for sovereignty, not yet a GitHub replacement.
review.best_for: OSS projects that care about censorship resistance and self-sovereign identity.
review.not_for: Teams that need a big audience, first-class CI, and turnkey collaboration.
